data.title: Amneal rated Buy in new research coverage at UBS as growth pipeline expands
data.description:
UBS on Friday initiated coverage of Amneal Pharmaceuticals (AMRX) with a Buy rating and a $19 price target, saying the stock offers one of the strongest growth profiles in specialty pharmaceuticals and could see a valuation re-rating after a recent pullback.

Shares had fallen about 14% over the prior two months, which UBS said created an attractive entry point for investors looking for a mix of stable base revenue and faster-growing products.

Parkinson’s portfolio seen as key growth driver

UBS analysts led by Ashwani Verma expect Amneal’s (AMRX) specialty segment to post a 6% compound annual growth rate from 2025 through 2030, ahead of the 4% consensus estimate. The firm pointed to strong uptake of Crexont, a Parkinson’s treatment launched in late 2024, and a slower-than-expected decline in older drug Rytary.

The bank estimates Crexont could generate about $440 million in sales by 2030, above Street forecasts near $390 million. UBS also said newly launched migraine therapy Brekiya could eventually reach roughly $80 million in peak sales.

Generics and biosimilars pipeline adds upside

UBS forecasts Amneal’s (AMRX) affordable medicines unit, which includes generics and biosimilars, will grow 9% annually through 2030, above the 7% consensus view. Analysts highlighted launches including iohexol, bimatoprost, sodium oxybate and a biosimilar version of Xolair.

The report also said upcoming patent expirations across branded pharmaceuticals could open an estimated $80 billion market opportunity for generic and biosimilar competitors through the next several years.

Margin expansion and lower leverage expected

UBS expects branded product growth to help expand operating margins by 300 basis points over five years. The bank also sees leverage improving, with net debt to ebitda falling below 3 times in the near term.

Valuation still modest, UBS says

At roughly 11 times expected 2027 earnings, UBS said the current valuation implies only modest future growth and does not fully reflect Amneal’s (AMRX) branded portfolio or upgraded generics strategy. Its $19 target is based on a 15 times multiple of projected 2027 earnings.

data.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn reuses booster but misses target orbit
data.description:
Blue Origin launched its New Glenn rocket on its third mission Sunday, successfully reusing a booster for the first time, but the payload failed to reach its planned orbit.

The rocket lifted off from Cape Canaveral, Florida, at about 7:25 a.m. local time. Roughly 10 minutes later, the reusable first-stage booster landed on a barge in the Atlantic Ocean.

Blue Origin said the satellite separated from the upper stage but entered an “off-nominal orbit,” meaning it was placed incorrectly. The company said the spacecraft has powered on and is being evaluated.

The mission carried a satellite for AST SpaceMobile (ASTS), which is building a direct-to-cellphone communications network. The Texas-based company had seven satellites in orbit entering 2026 and has said it hopes to launch as many as 60 this year.

The setback could complicate Blue Origin’s effort to establish New Glenn as a credible rival to SpaceX’s Falcon 9. The heavy-lift rocket has faced years of delays and slower-than-expected launch cadence.

Chief Executive Dave Limp said before the mission that Blue Origin hoped to complete eight to 12 launches this year, citing strong demand for satellite deployment.

Blue Origin managed only two New Glenn launches last year after initially projecting six to eight.

Beyond launch services, the company is expanding into satellite connectivity, orbital data centers and lunar missions. Limp also said Blue Origin’s Mark 1 lunar lander has “a very good chance” of reaching the moon later this year.

data.title: SA Asks: What are the most attractive communication services stocks?
data.description:
Earlier this week, Amazon (AMZN) announced it was acquiring Globalstar (GSAT) for $11.7B to help expand its burgeoning Leo satellite internet network, a potential rival to SpaceX's (SPACE) Starlink. 

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Kenio Fontes, Dividend Collection Agency, and Eugenio Catone which communication services stocks they thought were most attractive right now.

Kenio Fontes: Amazon (AMZN) isn’t the first company that comes to mind in the communications sector, but it’s certainly a company I really like right now. Unlike Verizon (VZ), T-Mobile US (TMUS), and similar companies, the risk of disruption is much lower here, and the company is much more diversified.

Amazon (AMZN) isn’t just e-commerce and cloud; it also has optionalities such as “space-based connectivity" and streaming with Prime Video. So the right way to look at this is as a comprehensive bet on a robust ecosystem with plenty of innovation (not just in communications). But mentioning Amazon is almost like cheating, since it's an “everything" company.

Another company in this specific sector that I recently bought into and am becoming more optimistic about is Meta (META). 

I’m not a big fan of Meta's reliance on ads and engagement on Instagram/Facebook, but gradually I’m seeing a more promising future for the company. The market reacted well to Meta Muse Spark, but I still think the consensus underestimates the long-term potential for AI integration. There’s truly a potential ecosystem here waiting to be built. 

WhatsApp is the best example. Monetization is low today, but there are billions of users who use it heavily—not only as a tool to chat with family and friends, but also as something every business, whether a local or Fortune 500 company, needs to be on in regions like Brazil and Europe. This creates room for various potential revenue streams from AI assistants, optimization/automation for businesses, and so on. 

Meta (META) even has opportunities in health, thanks to AI and the smart glasses ventures. And since the P/E ratio remains low (22x for an EPS growth of 20%+ in 2026), I still believe the market is underestimating Meta’s potential to sustainably expand both its top line and margins in the coming years.

Dividend Collection Agency: I believe T-Mobile US (TMUS) is the most attractive communication services stock right now. 

The stock has fallen more than 24% in the last year. I like the stock below $200, and they currently have a forward P/E less than 18x, well below their 5-year average of 32.57x. They also trade below their 5-year PEG ratio. While AT&T (T) and Verizon (VZ) are most attractive from a valuation standpoint, TMUS (TMUS) offers a better blend of growth and income. 

Once we have more economic clarity around inflation and interest rates trend lower, I see TMUS’s share price moving towards the $250-$300 range. 

Eugenio Catone: Among all the communication stocks that are part of my portfolio—Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Meta (META), Netflix (NFLX)—I believe that Netflix is currently the best opportunity for two key reasons. 

First, it is traded at a PE ratio ((FWD)) way lower than the 5-year historical average of 37.43x. While I am writing, the stock is falling 9% following the earnings release, and it is traded at a PE ratio ((FWD)) of just 30.50x; Meta and Google are both traded above their 5-year average PE ratio ((FWD)). 

Second, the ongoing energy crisis might cause a global decline in consumption, and both Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Meta (META) are deeply reliant on advertising spending, which is one of the first expenses that companies tend to cut when they face hard times. I see Netflix (NFLX) as a platform that no household wants to do without, even when disposable income drops. It is basically a must-have, and this will be reflected in resilient EPS regardless of the macroeconomic context.

  • Top Communication Services Stocks
  • Top Interactive Media and Services Stocks
  • Top Wireless Telecommunication Services Stocks
  • Top Integrated Telecommunications Services Stocks
  • Top Alternative Carrier Stocks

data.title: Iran to expand internet access for professors as public blackout continues
data.description:
Iran said it will widen internet access for university professors, state media reported Sunday, while most of the country remained under a sweeping internet shutdown now in its 51st day.

The blackout was imposed during Iran’s conflict with the United States and Israel, with authorities citing national security concerns. Since then, millions of Iranians have faced severe limits on communication, business activity and access to outside information.

Residents have largely been restricted to Iran’s domestic internet network, which blocks many foreign websites and is closely monitored. Broader international access has mainly been reserved for officials and select groups.

Authorities have eased some restrictions in recent days. Reports said Google Search and Google Maps had partially returned, though many linked websites and most global services remain inaccessible.

Deputy Science Minister Seyed Mehdi Abtahi said professors and researchers would gradually receive access to most online sites, excluding those still subject to censorship.

The move drew criticism from activists and some users, who said limited restoration for favored groups highlights growing inequality in digital access.

Critics warn Iran may be moving toward a “tiered internet” system, where reliable global access is available mainly to politically connected or state-approved users.

Digital rights advocates said the policy treats internet access as a security tool rather than a basic public right, the New York Times reported Sunday.

data.title: Hackers steal nearly $300M in biggest DeFi exploit of 2026
data.description:
Hackers drained nearly $300 million from a cross-chain crypto bridge on Saturday, triggering losses across several decentralized finance platforms in what appears to be the largest DeFi exploit of 2026, Bloomberg News reported Sunday.

The attacker stole about 116,500 rsETH, a token issued by Kelp DAO that represents restaked Ether, by targeting a bridge built with LayerZero technology, which connects different blockchains.

“We identified suspicious cross-chain activity involving rsETH,” Kelp DAO said in a post on X. “We have paused rsETH contracts across mainnet and several L2s while we investigate.”

Because rsETH is widely used across lending, trading and liquidity platforms, the breach quickly spread beyond a single protocol.

“This was not just a protocol exploit, it immediately became a cross-protocol contagion event,” security firm Cyvers said, estimating that at least nine platforms were affected.

Many DeFi systems rely on the same assets as collateral or liquidity, meaning a failure in one project can ripple through others.

“This is exactly the kind of incident that highlights the risks” of interconnected systems in DeFi, said Cyvers Chief Executive Deddy Lavid. “The challenge is no longer just preventing exploits at the contract level, but understanding how fast they can cascade across integrated protocols.”

Aave, the largest DeFi lending platform, froze markets tied to rsETH to limit further damage.

Cyvers Chief Technology Officer Meir Dolev said the exploit nearly became worse, adding the protocol was “just three minutes away from losing an additional $100 million” before a blacklist blocked a second attempt.

The breach exceeds an earlier attack on Solana-based Drift and comes at a fragile time for the crypto sector.

data.title: ‘Super Mario Galaxy Movie’ stays No. 1 as domestic box office slips 34%
data.description:
The domestic box office slowed this weekend, with total ticket sales estimated at $90.6 million, down 34% from the comparable weekend a year earlier, according to Comscore. Year-to-date domestic revenue reached $2.386 billion, up 17% from the same point in 2025.

Universal’s (CMCSA) “The Super Mario Galaxy Movie” held the top spot for a third straight weekend, collecting an estimated $35 million from 4,170 theaters. The animated release has now generated $355.2 million domestically and $747.5 million worldwide.

Amazon MGM’s (AMZN) “Project Hail Mary” remained in second place with $20.5 million in its fifth weekend, slipping just 15%. The Ryan Gosling-led science-fiction film has earned $285.1 million domestically and $573.1 million globally.

Warner Bros.’ (WBD) “Lee Cronin’s The Mummy” was the top new wide release, debuting in third place with $13.5 million from 3,304 locations. The R-rated horror reboot added $20.5 million overseas for a $34 million global opening.

A24’s “The Drama” landed fourth with $4.84 million in its third weekend, pushing its domestic total to $39.7 million. Universal’s “You, Me & Tuscany” rounded out the top five with $3.8 million in its second frame.

Among smaller debuts, Magnolia Pictures’ “Normal” opened in seventh place with $2.65 million, while indie comedy “Busboys” took in $1.61 million from 800 theaters. Indian horror-comedy “Bhooth Bangla” earned $977,582 after opening Thursday.

The weekend’s top 10 films generated a combined $86.6 million, according to studio estimates compiled by Comscore.

Top 10 Domestic Box Office (April 17-19, 2026)

  1. The Super Mario Galaxy Movie — $35.0 million
  2. Project Hail Mary — $20.456 million
  3. Lee Cronin’s The Mummy — $13.515 million
  4. The Drama — $4.842 million
  5. You, Me & Tuscany — $3.8 million
  6. Hoppers — $2.9 million
  7. Normal — $2.65 million
  8. Busboys — $1.614 million
  9. Bhooth Bangla — $977,582
  10. A Great Awakening — $823,667

data.title: QXO agrees to buy TopBuild in $17B building products deal
data.description:
QXO Inc. said it has agreed to acquire TopBuild Corp. in a transaction valued at about $17 billion, a move that would broaden its presence across several building products categories in North America.

The deal has been approved by both companies’ boards and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, pending shareholder approval and other customary conditions.

TopBuild is a major distributor and installer of insulation and related products. QXO said combining its roofing, waterproofing and lumber-related operations with TopBuild’s insulation business would create a larger company with a broader product mix and higher-margin service offerings.

The acquisition follows QXO’s recent purchase of Kodiak Building Partners for about $2.25 billion, which closed earlier this month. After the TopBuild transaction, QXO said it expects to compete in an addressable market of more than $300 billion.

Under the terms of the agreement, TopBuild shareholders may elect to receive either $505 in cash or 20.2 shares of QXO stock for each TopBuild share, subject to proration so that approximately 45% of the total consideration is paid in cash and 55% in QXO shares.

The offer values TopBuild shares at a 19.8% premium to the company’s 60-day volume-weighted average price and a 23.1% premium to its April 17 closing price, according to the companies.

TopBuild generated about $6.2 billion in 2025 net sales and about $1.14 billion in adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, adjusted for acquisitions completed during the year, the release said. QXO said it expects roughly $300 million in synergies from the integration by 2030.

Following completion of the transaction, QXO said the combined company would have about 28,000 employees, 1,150 locations across the U.S. and Canada, and a fleet of more than 10,000 vehicles.

data.title: Iran rejects second round of U.S. talks, cites ‘excessive’ American demands
data.description:
Iran will not participate in a second round of talks with the United States, according to Iranian state media, which said Tehran objected to what it described as excessive and unrealistic demands from Washington.

The reported decision casts doubt on efforts to revive diplomacy after recent high-level contacts between the two countries in Pakistan, where negotiators had sought to ease tensions over Iran’s nuclear program, regional security issues and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

Iranian media cites shifting U.S. positions

Iran’s official IRNA news agency said repeated changes in U.S. positions, contradictory public statements and unreasonable requests had blocked progress in the negotiations.

Iranian officials have recently criticized Washington’s “maximalist” stance, particularly over uranium enrichment and broader security demands.

U.S. still signals interest in diplomacy

Despite Tehran’s announcement, President Donald Trump said U.S. negotiators were prepared to continue talks and planned to travel to Pakistan for another round of meetings.

The White House has publicly maintained that diplomacy remains possible, even as Trump has paired that message with renewed threats of military action if no agreement is reached.

Markets watching Strait of Hormuz

The diplomatic setback comes as investors monitor disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global oil shipments. Any prolonged impasse between Washington and Tehran could intensify concerns over energy supplies and regional stability.

data.title: More Americans are tapping 401(k)s early as household costs squeeze budgets
data.description:
A growing number of Americans are withdrawing money from their 401(k) retirement accounts before retirement age as rising living costs and tighter household budgets force difficult financial choices, the New York Times reported Saturday.

Six percent of Vanguard retirement plan participants took hardship withdrawals in 2025, up from 4.8% in 2024 and 3.6% in 2023. Vanguard said the rate is roughly triple pre-pandemic levels. The median withdrawal last year was $1,900. Fidelity and Charles Schwab also reported increases.

The trend comes as many households grapple with higher prices for groceries, housing, utilities and transportation, along with elevated borrowing costs. According to Intuit Credit Karma, more than half of Americans either made or planned early withdrawals from retirement accounts in 2025 or 2026 because of affordability concerns.

Hardship withdrawals are generally allowed only for qualifying needs such as medical bills, preventing eviction or foreclosure, or certain home-related expenses. In many cases, workers under age 59½ still owe income taxes plus a 10% early-withdrawal penalty.

Recent law changes have made access easier. The 2018 Bipartisan Budget Act removed the requirement that many workers first take a 401(k) loan before requesting a hardship withdrawal. The SECURE 2.0 Act later allowed penalty-free withdrawals of up to $1,000 annually for some emergency expenses if repayment rules are met.

Financial advisers warn that repeated early withdrawals can significantly reduce long-term retirement savings by shrinking balances that would otherwise compound over time. The increase in withdrawals also highlights a broader retirement savings gap, with many workers already behind on preparing for retirement.

data.title: SA Asks: Is HP an attractive takeover target?
data.description:
HP (HPQ) shares rallied earlier this week on a report that Nvidia (NVDA) was in talks to acquire a PC maker, a rumor that Nvidia later denied.

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts IWA Research and Gary Alexander if they thought HP was an attractive takeover target.

IWA Research: HPQ is attractive from several points of view, which is why it wouldn't be impossible to see them garner the attention of a buyer. The company's foundation remains very solid, with stable and strong cash flow. It's been able to quickly respond to the initial tariff headwinds through supply chain optimizations, proving its ability to navigate what is otherwise a very tough market while still delivering stable FCF that allowed them to return double-digit combined (dividend plus buybacks) yields. 

In the longer term, there's plenty of upside potential from a new, inevitable hardware replacement cycle, and their AI PC investments could help them capture even more market share. Even without an acquisition, HPQ's attractive valuation and strong foundation are major reasons why I rated the company Strong Buy in March.

Although Nvidia (NVDA) denied being in talks, HPQ's valuation remains very compelling, to the point where there could be multiple other buyers that would show interest. 

First, there could be particular interest from private equity or investment firms such as Blackstone (BX), KKR (KKR), or even Silver Lake, which took Dell (DELL) private over a decade ago before taking them public again, a clear display of what private equity can do in this space and highlighting the fact that they're paying attention to it. 

I also wouldn't rule out the possibility of seeing a giant like Samsung (SSNLF), which sold their printer business to HP about nine years ago, or Qualcomm (QCOM) showing interest. HP's position in the PC market and particularly the enterprise space remains very strong, with potential for monumental synergies in case a major hardware producer acquires them.

Gary Alexander: I don't believe HPQ as a whole was a palatable M&A target for Nvidia. The issue is the lagging print division, which is ~60% of HP's operating profit and ostensibly the larger share of the overall company's enterprise value. I find it unlikely that a market darling like Nvidia would want to attach itself to a legacy business in secular decline. 

That said, Nvidia could make a play for a carve-out of the company's PC division, which has seen a recent acceleration in sales and gives Nvidia a chance to vertically integrate its GPU business with a PC OEM with compelling market share.

  • Top Technology Hardware, Storage, and Peripherals Stocks
